title: General Knowledge Night
description: A stronger general knowledge round with an Indian quiz-night flavour across history, culture, science, literature, and geography.

questions.prompt: Which Indian scientist is most closely associated with leading the Green Revolution in India?
questions.hint: He was an agricultural scientist from Tamil Nadu.

questions.prompt: Who was the first Indian to win an individual Olympic gold medal?
questions.hint: He won in shooting at the 2008 Beijing Olympics.

questions.prompt: Who wrote the Hindi novel 'Godan'?
questions.hint: He is often called the 'Upanyas Samrat' of Hindi literature.

questions.prompt: Which classical dance form developed in Tamil Nadu and grew out of the old temple dance tradition?
questions.hint: Its modern recital format often begins with an alarippu.

questions.prompt: On the banks of which river are the ruins of Hampi located?
questions.hint: It is an important tributary of the Krishna.

questions.prompt: Who founded the Arya Samaj?
questions.hint: He wrote 'Satyarth Prakash'.

questions.prompt: Who wrote the novel 'Anandamath'?
questions.hint: He also wrote 'Vande Mataram'.

questions.prompt: Which article of the Indian Constitution abolishes untouchability?
questions.hint: It is a short article in the Fundamental Rights section.

questions.prompt: Sriharikota, India's main satellite launch centre, is in which state?
questions.hint: Its capital is Amaravati.

questions.prompt: Which Indian mathematician worked with G. H. Hardy and left behind famous notebooks?
questions.hint: He came from Kumbakonam.

questions.prompt: Who wrote the novel 'Train to Pakistan'?
questions.hint: He was also a well-known journalist and columnist.

questions.prompt: The Sun Temple at Konark is in which present-day state?
questions.hint: Its capital is Bhubaneswar.

questions.prompt: Who is known as the architect of Operation Flood?
questions.hint: He is also known as the father of the White Revolution in India.

questions.prompt: The Indian Naval Academy at Ezhimala is in which state?
questions.hint: Its capital is Thiruvananthapuram.

questions.prompt: Which city is often called the 'Manchester of South India'?
questions.hint: It is a major textile city in Tamil Nadu.

questions.prompt: Who founded Santiniketan?
questions.hint: He was India's first Nobel laureate.

questions.prompt: Who was the first woman Chief Justice of a state High Court in India?
questions.hint: She later chaired the Justice Verma Committee on sexual assault laws.

questions.prompt: The Ajanta caves are especially famous for which kind of art?
questions.hint: They are painted on walls and ceilings rather than carved as statues alone.

questions.prompt: Which constitutional amendment is often called the 'Mini-Constitution'?
questions.hint: It was passed during the Emergency.

questions.prompt: Kalaripayattu is traditionally associated with which state?
questions.hint: It is on India's southwest coast.

questions.prompt: Which Mughal prince translated the Upanishads into Persian?
questions.hint: He was executed in the war of succession by his brother Aurangzeb.

questions.prompt: On the banks of which river does Nashik stand?
questions.hint: It is sometimes called the Dakshin Ganga.

questions.prompt: Who painted the famous nationalist image 'Bharat Mata' in 1905?
questions.hint: He was associated with the Bengal School of Art.

questions.prompt: Who was the first Indian chess player to become World Champion?
questions.hint: He is from Tamil Nadu.

questions.prompt: Who founded the Brahmo Samaj?
questions.hint: He campaigned strongly against sati.

questions.prompt: Which physicist gave his name to Bose-Einstein statistics along with Albert Einstein?
questions.hint: His initials were S. N.

questions.prompt: Who founded Banaras Hindu University?
questions.hint: He was also called Mahamana.

questions.prompt: Majuli, one of the world's best-known river islands, lies in which river?
questions.hint: It flows through Assam.

questions.prompt: Who wrote the novel 'A Fine Balance'?
questions.hint: He was born in Mumbai and later lived in Canada.

questions.prompt: Sattriya, now recognised as a classical dance form, originated in which state?
questions.hint: It is linked with the Vaishnav monasteries called satras.

questions.prompt: In which language was Rabindranath Tagore's 'Gitanjali' originally written?
questions.hint: It was later self-translated into English selections.

questions.prompt: In which city is the Victoria Memorial located?
questions.hint: It was once the capital of British India.

questions.prompt: The ruins of the ancient university of Nalanda are in which state?
questions.hint: Its capital is Patna.

questions.prompt: Which Indian language was the first to be granted classical language status by the Government of India?
questions.hint: It is one of the longest continuously attested classical traditions in Asia.

questions.prompt: Yakshagana is a traditional dance-drama form from which state?
questions.hint: It is especially associated with the coastal districts around Udupi and Dakshina Kannada.

questions.prompt: Who wrote the Marathi novel 'Mrityunjaya', based on Karna?
questions.hint: He was from Kolhapur.

questions.prompt: Rani ki Vav, a UNESCO World Heritage stepwell, is in which state?
questions.hint: It is in Patan.

questions.prompt: In which city is the Indian Military Academy located?
questions.hint: It is in the Doon Valley.

questions.prompt: Which national park is especially associated with the conservation of the hard-ground barasingha?
questions.hint: It is in Madhya Pradesh.

questions.prompt: Who planned much of the modern city of Chandigarh?
questions.hint: He was a Swiss-French architect.

questions.prompt: Jallianwala Bagh is in which city?
questions.hint: It is home to the Harmandir Sahib.

questions.prompt: Who wrote the modern history book 'India After Gandhi'?
questions.hint: He is also known for writing on environmental and social history.

questions.prompt: Who was the first recipient of the Param Vir Chakra?
questions.hint: He was awarded it posthumously for action in the 1947-48 war.

questions.prompt: The Zero Mile marker associated with the geographical centre of colonial India is in which city?
questions.hint: It is in Maharashtra and is famous for oranges.

questions.prompt: Odissi as a classical dance form developed in which state?
questions.hint: The Konark and Jagannath traditions are closely tied to it.

questions.prompt: SEBI, the capital markets regulator of India, is headquartered in which city?
questions.hint: It is India's financial capital.

questions.prompt: Who is often called the father of the Indian space programme?
questions.hint: He founded the Physical Research Laboratory in Ahmedabad.

questions.prompt: The Great Stupa at Sanchi is in which state?
questions.hint: Its capital is Bhopal.

questions.prompt: Rashtrapati Bhavan in New Delhi was primarily designed by which architect?
questions.hint: New Delhi's plan is strongly associated with his surname.

questions.prompt: What is the traditional name for the confluence of the Ganga, Yamuna, and the mythical Saraswati at Prayagraj?
questions.hint: It begins with T.
